section_miscategorizations.text: He has had a baby with intestinal surgery at one month old.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a past surgical history detail (objective medical history) rather than a current subjective symptom or concern; however, it does not substantially mislead the clinical meaning.

section_miscategorizations.text: Physical exam reveals well-healed abdominal surgical scars.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The presence of abdominal surgical scars implies a prior surgery that is not otherwise documented; this would more cleanly belong in past history, but its placement in O as an exam finding is acceptable and only a minor organizational issue.

section_miscategorizations.text: A trial of proton pump inhibitors is warranted.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a management decision (treatment plan) rather than diagnostic assessment; it should be documented as part of the plan, not the assessment.

section_miscategorizations.text: An upper endoscopy is recommended to evaluate for underlying causes of GERD.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an order/recommendation for a diagnostic procedure and belongs in the plan section, not in the assessment.
The wording suggests a continuation or renewal of an existing medication, which is appropriate in P as a treatment change/order; no true miscategorization, but the intent could be clearer.
